skill.name: Market Opportunity Analysis
skill.description: Generate comprehensive market opportunity analysis with TAM/SAM/SOM calculations
skill.summary: Interactive market sizing skill for startups using bottom-up and top-down methodologies
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security issues detected. The skill provides structured guidance for market opportunity analysis using Claude's built-in tools (Read, Write, WebSearch, WebFetch, Grep, Glob, Bash). No dangerous patterns, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration risks identified.

content.user_title: Generate market opportunity analysis with TAM SAM SOM calculations
content.value_statement: Startup founders and analysts need credible market sizing to secure funding and guide strategy. This skill provides structured methodologies for calculating Total Addressable Market, Serviceable Available Market, and Serviceable Obtainable Market using both bottom-up and top-down approaches.
actual_capabilities: Guide interactive market sizing conversations with structured questions
Calculate TAM using bottom-up methodology for B2B, SaaS, and consumer markets
Validate market size with top-down analysis using industry reports
Compute SAM by applying geographic and product filters
Estimate realistic SOM projections for 3-5 year horizons
Generate comprehensive markdown reports with documented assumptions and cited sources
limitations: Market data quality depends on available public sources and may have gaps
Cannot access paid research databases or proprietary industry reports
Calculations are estimates based on user-provided information and public data
Does not replace professional market research consulting for critical decisions

use_cases.title: Seed Stage Pitch Deck Preparation
use_cases.description: Generate credible TAM SAM SOM numbers for investor pitch decks with documented methodologies and sources
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders raising seed or Series A funding

use_cases.title: New Product Market Validation
use_cases.description: Assess market opportunity before committing resources to product development
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and business development teams

use_cases.title: Strategic Planning and Market Entry
use_cases.description: Evaluate market size and growth potential for expansion decisions

prompt_templates.title: Quick Market Sizing
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me estimate the market size for [product/service]. My target customers are [customer description] in [geography]. My pricing model is [pricing]. Calculate TAM using a simple bottom-up approach.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive TAM SAM SOM Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a full market opportunity analysis for [product/service]. Include bottom-up TAM calculation, top-down validation, SAM with filters, and SOM projections for years 3 and 5. Document all assumptions and cite sources.

prompt_templates.title: Investor-Ready Market Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an investor-ready market sizing report for [company] targeting [market]. Include executive summary, detailed methodologies, segment breakdowns, competitive context, and sanity checks against public company benchmarks.

prompt_templates.title: Market Comparison Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the market opportunity for [product] across three different customer segments: [segment 1], [segment 2], and [segment 3]. Calculate TAM for each and recommend which segment to target first.

output_examples.input: AI-powered email marketing for e-commerce companies with $1M+ revenue in North America, $300/month average subscription
output_examples.output: TAM: $2.1B (70,000 e-commerce companies x $3,600 ACV), SAM: $840M (40% North America x 50% email marketing adoption), SOM Year 3: $16.8M (2% of SAM), SOM Year 5: $42M (5% of SAM)

output_examples.input: HR software for mid-market healthcare organizations in the US
output_examples.output: TAM: $1.4B (28,000 healthcare orgs x $50K ACV), SAM: $560M (US-only x 40% mid-market focus), SOM Year 3: $11.2M (2% penetration), Validation: 30% variance from Gartner HR tech report

best_practices: Start with bottom-up analysis for credibility, then validate with top-down data
Document every assumption and cite all data sources with URLs and dates
Be conservative on SOM estimates to maintain credibility with investors
anti_patterns: Relying solely on top-down market reports without bottom-up validation
Cherry-picking optimistic data sources to inflate market size numbers
Claiming more than 10 percent SOM without strong competitive justification

faq.question: What is the difference between TAM, SAM, and SOM?
faq.answer: TAM (Total Addressable Market) is the total revenue opportunity if you had 100 percent market share. SAM (Serviceable Available Market) is the portion of TAM you can realistically serve. SOM (Serviceable Obtainable Market) is the share you can realistically capture in 3-5 years.

faq.question: Which methodology is better: bottom-up or top-down?
faq.answer: Bottom-up is more credible because it builds from actual customer counts and pricing. Top-down is useful for validation. Investors prefer bottom-up because it demonstrates understanding of your specific market dynamics.

faq.question: How do I find reliable market data?
faq.answer: Start with government sources like Census and BLS, then industry reports from Gartner or Forrester, public company 10-K filings, trade associations, and academic research. Always cite sources with publication dates.

faq.question: What SOM percentage is realistic for a startup?
faq.answer: For most startups, 2-3 percent SOM by year 3 and 4-6 percent by year 5 is realistic. Claims above 10 percent require strong justification about competitive advantages and go-to-market capabilities.

faq.question: How long does a market sizing analysis take?
faq.answer: A thorough analysis typically takes 30-60 minutes depending on data availability. Complex markets with limited public data may take longer. Quality matters more than speed for investor-facing analysis.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-US markets?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works for any geographic market. Data availability may vary by country, but the methodologies remain the same. Specify your target geography clearly during the analysis.
